info.description: The per-tenant workspace-server HTTP API. Single source of truth for workspace/schedule/agent/secrets/files/memory CRUD. Hand-written clients (canvas, molecule-mcp-server, molecule-cli, molecule-ai-sdk) should be replaced by clients generated from this spec — see RFC #1706.
info.title: Molecule AI Workspace Server API

post.summary: Create or repair the org platform agent (concierge), core-only

get.summary: Get the org's display name + billing capability
List pending requests across the org (canvas tabs)
Get a request with its message thread

post.summary: Add a message to a request's More-Info thread
Respond to a request (done / rejected / approved)

get.summary: List pending user tasks across all workspaces

parameters.description: Partial schedule fields (only provided keys are updated)
parameters.name: body

get.summary: Get past runs of a schedule
List a workspace's own user tasks

delete.summary: Delete a workspace's own user task

patch.summary: Update a workspace's own user task

parameters.description: Partial task fields (only provided keys are updated)
parameters.name: body

name.description: Name is the org's display name (MOLECULE_ORG_NAME, "" when unset).

org_id.description:
OrgID is the org's UUID (MOLECULE_ORG_ID, "" when unset). Empty on a
self-hosted stack where no control plane assigns an org id.

platform_managed_available.description:
PlatformManagedAvailable reports whether a Molecule LLM proxy is wired
into this workspace-server process — i.e. whether platform_managed billing
can actually work. True on SaaS (the CP provisioner exports the proxy base
URL + usage token), false on a self-hosted stack (no hosted proxy / no
credit ledger). The canvas reads this pre-login to decide whether to offer
the "Platform (proxy)" billing option or hide it and default to BYOK.

slug.description:
Slug is the org's URL slug (MOLECULE_ORG_SLUG, "" when unset). Empty on
a self-hosted stack where no control plane assigns a slug.

force.description:
Force repairs (reinstall + re-provision) even a healthy 'online' concierge —
the explicit "repair tool" path. Default false keeps the call idempotent.

model.description:
Model, when set, is validated against the effective runtime and written as
the MODEL workspace_secret BEFORE the provision trigger fires, so the very
first provision resolves this pick instead of racing it (core#3496 — the
self-host onboarding scene's create path). Optional + additive: absent
keeps today's behavior (the platform default resolution at provision).

name.description: Name is the concierge display name; defaults to defaultPlatformAgentName().

runtime.description:
Runtime is the concierge runtime; empty falls back to the platform default
(conciergeDefaultRuntime), exactly like the CP-driven install. Used only on
a fresh insert — an existing root's runtime is preserved (runtime changes
go through the standard PATCH path). Must be a container-backed runtime:
external-like/mock/unknown values are rejected 422 (platformRuntimeAllowed).

BearerAuth.description: Bearer token issued by Gitea (org-admin or persona PAT) or by the platform's signup/SSO flow.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization

OrgIdAuth.description: Tenant routing header (UUID form). Alternative to X-Molecule-Org-Slug. At least one of OrgSlugAuth or OrgIdAuth must be sent alongside BearerAuth.
OrgIdAuth.name: X-Molecule-Org-Id

OrgSlugAuth.description: Tenant routing header — required on every /workspaces/{id}/* request so the platform edge can route to the correct per-tenant workspace-server. Either X-Molecule-Org-Slug (human-readable, e.g. "agents-team") or X-Molecule-Org-Id (UUID) must be sent; slug is preferred for client code.
OrgSlugAuth.name: X-Molecule-Org-Slug
